headlines.headline: Audio Captures Desperate Plea from Indian Tanker Captain Under Iranian Fire in Hormuz
headlines.summary: Audio recordings reveal the moment an Indian oil tanker captain begged 'let me turn back' as Iranian forces opened fire in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran fired on two Indian-flagged vessels, prompting New Delhi to summon Tehran's ambassador in protest.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu 'Shocked' Trump Prohibited Israel Strikes on Lebanon, Seeks Clarification
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu was reportedly shocked by Trump's public remarks prohibiting strikes on Lebanon, believing the statement exceeded the terms of the US-backed ceasefire. Israeli officials are seeking clarification from Washington.

headlines.headline: Five Loaded Qatari LNG Vessels Approach Strait of Hormuz as Closure Looms
headlines.summary: Five vessels loaded with liquefied natural gas from Qatar's Ras Laffan terminal are approaching the Strait of Hormuz, ship-tracking data showed. The vessels face uncertainty as Iran reimposed restrictions on the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: WFP Warns Over Half of Haiti's Population Faces Worsening Hunger Crisis
headlines.summary: The World Food Programme warned that more than 5.8 million Haitians — approximately 52% of the country's population — are facing acute hunger as the crisis-wracked Caribbean nation struggles with violence and political instability.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV Says 'Not in My Interest' to Debate Trump, Will Keep Preaching Peace
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV said it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate President Trump about the war in Iran but that he will continue preaching the Gospel message of peace. The Pope spoke to reporters during his Africa tour in Angola.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Deal Still Far from Reach as Major Gaps Remain on Enrichment
headlines.summary: Analysis suggests a US-Iran deal remains distant despite ongoing negotiations. Major gaps persist on uranium enrichment levels, while Iran uses Hormuz leverage to push for security guarantees and sanctions relief.

headlines.headline: IMF Warns Global Economy on Brink of Recession Amid Wars and Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund issued a stark warning that the global economy is 'threatened with being thrown off course' due to ongoing conflicts and soaring energy prices. The report highlights growing stagflation risks as the Iran war disrupts oil supplies and markets worldwide.

headlines.headline: US Lawmaker Launches Investigation into Jared Kushner's Business Ties and Diplomatic Role
headlines.summary: Democratic Representative Jamie Raskin opened a 'sweeping' investigation into President Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner over potential conflicts of interest. Kushner has been mediating Ukraine-Russia and Iran negotiations while running a private equity firm with potential financial stakes in the outcomes.

headlines.headline: CENTCOM Reveals 13 US Troops Killed, Nearly 400 Wounded in Iran War
headlines.summary: The US Central Command disclosed that 13 American troops have been killed and nearly 400 wounded in the Iran conflict. CENTCOM spokesman Tim Hawkins said 354 of the wounded have returned to duty, providing the first comprehensive casualty count since the war began.

headlines.headline: HSBC and Standard Chartered Linked to Multimillion-Pound Iran Money Laundering Scheme
headlines.summary: Two of Britain's largest banks have become embroiled in allegations of a multimillion-pound scheme to launder money for Iran. The allegations come as Western nations maintain strict sanctions on Tehran amid the ongoing conflict over the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: UK Startup to Test Acoustic Sensor System Against Russian Drones in Ukraine
headlines.summary: British defense startup Arcani plans to test its HARK acoustic sensor system in Ukraine, marking another instance of Western defense technology being deployed in the conflict. The passive acoustic reconnaissance kit is designed to detect hostile drones and incoming threats.

headlines.headline: US Forces Turn Back 23 Iranian Vessels as Naval Blockade Tightens
headlines.summary: The US military reported that 23 ships have complied with orders to turn back toward Iran since the American blockade of Iranian ports began. The enforcement action is part of the 'Economic Fury' campaign to pressure Tehran over the Strait of Hormuz closure.

headlines.headline: Trump Signs Executive Order Speeding Psychedelic Drug Reviews for PTSD Treatment
headlines.summary: President Trump directed his administration to expedite reviews of psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine, for treating mental health conditions. The order follows advocacy from veterans groups and conservative lawmakers seeking alternatives for PTSD treatment.

headlines.headline: Trump Organization Plans 70-Story Skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia
headlines.summary: The Trump Organization and partners plan to build a 70-story 'Trump Tower' in Georgia's capital, the Wall Street Journal reported. The project comes as President Trump faces scrutiny over potential conflicts between his business interests and foreign policy decisions.

headlines.headline: Venezuela's Machado Says 'No Regrets' About Giving Nobel Prize to Trump
headlines.summary: Exiled opposition leader María Corina Machado defended her decision to symbolically gift her Nobel Peace Prize to Trump, calling him a leader who 'risked the lives of his country's citizens' for Venezuela's freedom. She remains in contact with the Trump administration about returning to Venezuela.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV Challenges Angola's Leaders to End Africa's Exploitation
headlines.summary: During his Africa tour, Pope Leo XIV called on Angola's leaders to 'break the cycles of interests' that have plundered and exploited Africa, blasting 'despots and tyrants' who make false promises. He dismissed media narratives of a rift with Trump over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: India's Defence Minister Reviews West Asia Situation Amid Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh chaired a meeting in New Delhi to assess India's preparedness as the Strait of Hormuz crisis escalates. The review comes after Iran fired on two Indian tankers and India approved a $1.4 billion maritime insurance pool.

headlines.headline: Trump Convenes Situation Room Briefing as Iran Chokes Off World Oil Supply
headlines.summary: President Trump called an emergency meeting with his inner circle, including JD Vance, Marco Rubio, and Pete Hegseth, to discuss the Strait of Hormuz crisis. The briefing came after Iran reimposed restrictions on the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: Trump, IRS in Talks to Settle President's $10 Billion Lawsuit Over Leaked Tax Returns
headlines.summary: Lawyers for Donald Trump and the IRS are negotiating a settlement of the president's $10 billion lawsuit against the tax agency for leaking his tax returns to media outlets in 2019 and 2020. The case has drawn attention to privacy protections for taxpayer information.

headlines.headline: Japan Embraces Nuclear Power Post-Fukushima as AI Drives Energy Demand
headlines.summary: Japan is rapidly moving to restart nuclear reactors as artificial intelligence increases electricity demand and foreign wars reshape energy security thinking. The country is reconsidering its post-Fukushima nuclear phase-out as it faces growing power needs.

headlines.headline: Iran's Resistance Offers Lessons for Taiwan, Security Analysts Say
headlines.summary: Iran's ability to prevent a decisive US victory despite inferior weapons has implications for Taiwan's defense strategy. Analysts note that decentralized command, dispersed weapons, and large drone/missile stockpiles helped Iran close the Strait of Hormuz and withstand strikes.

headlines.headline: Kyiv Gunman Was Russian-Born, Zelenskyy Calls Shooting 'Act of Terrorism'
headlines.summary: Ukraine's security service labeled the Kyiv supermarket attack as terrorism after the 58-year-old gunman was identified as Russian-born. The shooter had a valid weapons permit, prior criminal record, and had lived in the Donetsk region before killing six and wounding 14.

headlines.headline: Trump Says China's Xi Is 'Very Happy' About Hormuz Status Ahead of China Visit
headlines.summary: President Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping was 'very happy' about developments with the Strait of Hormuz and reiterated he looks forward to their meeting in China next month. The trip was delayed from March due to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Russia Strikes Ukrainian Port and Power Grid, Ukraine Hits Russian Oil Facilities
headlines.summary: Russian attacks overnight damaged port infrastructure in Ukraine's Odesa region and caused blackouts for 380,000 consumers in the north. Ukraine struck back with drone attacks on oil refineries in Samara, a Crimea oil depot, and a Baltic Sea petroleum port.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV Condemns 'Despots and Tyrants' Exploiting Africa During Angola Visit
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV sharply decried the exploitation of natural resources in Africa during a visit to Angola, blasting 'despots and tyrants' who guarantee wealth but deliver suffering. He also played down any rift with Trump over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: New Zealand Defends Military Patrol Flight Near China After Beijing's Protest
headlines.summary: New Zealand defended the actions of a military patrol aircraft flight near China after Beijing said it had undermined its security interests. The incident highlights growing tensions in the Pacific as Western nations increase surveillance operations.

headlines.headline: US Renews Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver Amid Iran War Price Shocks
headlines.summary: The Trump administration renewed a waiver allowing countries to buy sanctioned Russian oil at sea for about a month, responding to pressure from nations dealing with oil price volatility from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Six Killed in Kyiv Shooting and Hostage Incident, Police Neutralize Gunman
headlines.summary: A gunman opened fire on passersby in a Kyiv district before barricading himself in a supermarket with hostages. Ukrainian police killed the suspect, but six people died in the incident.

headlines.headline: India Approves $1.4 Billion Maritime Insurance Pool Amid War Disruptions
headlines.summary: India approved a 129.8-billion-rupee guarantee for a maritime insurance pool as wars and sanctions prompt insurers to withdraw cover, threatening trade flows. The move comes after Indian tankers were fired upon in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: US Mine-Clearing Capabilities Questioned as Strait of Hormuz Threat Looms
headlines.summary: Analysts are raising doubts about America's ability to clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting a gap in US naval capabilities and reinforcing the need for NATO allies in mine countermeasure operations.

headlines.headline: Israel Enforces 'Yellow Line' Buffer Zone in Lebanon, Strikes Approaching Individuals
headlines.summary: The Israeli army publicly referred to a 'Yellow Line' for the first time, described as a self-declared buffer zone in Lebanon. Israel said it targeted individuals approaching the zone, marking an escalation in ceasefire enforcement.

headlines.headline: French Peacekeeper Killed in Lebanon Attack During Ceasefire
headlines.summary: A French UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed and three others wounded in a small arms attack near Ghandouriyeh in southern Lebanon. French President Macron blamed Hezbollah, which denied involvement. The attack came just after a 10-day Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire took effect.

headlines.headline: Trump Predicts 'Special' Meeting with Xi Jinping Ahead of China Visit
headlines.summary: President Trump said he looks forward to meeting President Xi Jinping in China next month, predicting the summit would be 'historic' and achieve much. The trip was delayed from March due to the Iran war. Analysts say the meeting could stabilize China-US relations through 2029.

headlines.headline: China Could Gain 'Massive Strategic Advantage' from Iran War, Analyst Says
headlines.summary: A Chinese analyst said Beijing should draw military lessons from the Iran conflict, noting that US engagement in warfare narrows the equipment gap between Chinese and American forces. With the ceasefire likely to extend, China may assume a more active role in peace talks.

headlines.headline: Venezuela's Machado Draws Thousands in Madrid, Declines Meeting with Spain's PM
headlines.summary: Exiled Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado rallied thousands in Madrid while PM Sánchez hosted a progressive summit in Barcelona. Machado praised Trump for removing Maduro and said she declined meeting Sánchez because of the leftist gathering.

headlines.headline: Iran Says No Date Set for Next Round of US Nuclear Negotiations
headlines.summary: Iran's deputy foreign minister said no date has been set for the next round of negotiations with the United States, adding that a framework of understanding must be agreed first before face-to-face talks can resume.

headlines.headline: India Summons Iranian Envoy After Navy Fires on Indian Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Two Indian-flagged vessels, including a crude oil tanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi oil, were fired upon by the Iranian Navy north of Oman. New Delhi lodged a strong protest with Iran's envoy, noting Iran had previously promised not to target friendly nations' ships.

headlines.headline: US Military Prepares to Board and Seize Iran-Linked Ships Worldwide
headlines.summary: The Pentagon is planning to intercept and take control of commercial vessels tied to Iran, potentially far beyond the Middle East, as part of an economic pressure campaign dubbed 'Economic Fury' aimed at forcing Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and make concessions in nuclear talks.

headlines.headline: Progressive Leaders Rally in Barcelona to Defend Multilateral Order Against Trump's 'Unilateralism'
headlines.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez hosted progressive leaders from Brazil, Mexico, South Africa and other nations in Barcelona, with attendees warning that attacks on the multilateral system and international law have become normalized. Trump attacked Spain on social media during the summit.

headlines.headline: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again as Ships Mid-Transit Reverse Course
headlines.summary: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again, with maritime tracking showing ships abandoning attempts to pass through the vital waterway. The move came after the US maintained its naval blockade of Iranian ports, casting doubt on Trump's optimism about an imminent peace deal.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Iran 'Got a Little Cute' by Re-blocking Hormuz, Claims Talks Going 'Really Well'
headlines.summary: President Trump downplayed Iran's reversal on the Strait of Hormuz, saying the Islamic Republic 'got a little cute' but negotiations remain on track. Egypt and Pakistan said they hope to secure a final agreement 'in the coming days' as a 10-day ceasefire nears expiration.

headlines.headline: Turkey Warns US Withdrawal from European Security Would Be 'Destructive'
headlines.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said discussions are underway on managing a possible US withdrawal from European security architecture, calling the potential move 'destructive' for regional stability.

headlines.headline: Iran Reimposes Restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, Vessels Report Gunfire
headlines.summary: Iran has reimposed 'strict control' on the Strait of Hormuz just one day after announcing its reopening, with shipping sources reporting at least two vessels came under fire as the waterway closed again.

headlines.headline: Saudi Finance Minister: Oil-Producing Countries Need Time to Ramp Up Output
headlines.summary: Saudi Finance Minister Mohammed Al-Jadaan welcomed Iran's reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but warned it will take time for oil-producing nations to increase output, noting the Middle East situation remains 'very fragile' until a durable de-escalation is achieved.

headlines.headline: The Iran War Has Revealed Trump's Pressure Point: The Economy
headlines.summary: Iran has taken a beating militarily but demonstrated it can exact economic costs that Trump and his aides underestimated, raising the political risk back home and creating a new dynamic in the conflict.

headlines.headline: Displaced Lebanese Return Home as Ceasefire Is Tested
headlines.summary: More displaced Lebanese are heading home as the ceasefire appears to mostly hold, though Israel says it carried out strikes on what it called 'terrorists' approaching the border area.

headlines.headline: Sanctioned Tankers Cross Strait of Hormuz Before Shots Fired
headlines.summary: Ship-tracking data shows at least three sanctioned vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz before Iran reimposed restrictions and gunfire was reported at vessels in the waterway.

headlines.headline: Iranian Official Says US 'Maximalist' Demands Stall Face-to-Face Talks
headlines.summary: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister told the AP on the margins of a Turkey diplomacy forum that maximalist US demands are preventing face-to-face negotiations, with no date yet set for a new round of talks.

headlines.headline: Climate Now Embedded in Geopolitics and Energy Policy, Says Former COP30 Official
headlines.summary: Climate has moved beyond a 'niche theme' and is now embedded in geopolitics, energy, and global policy discussions, said a former COP30 official in an interview with Anadolu Agency.
